question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the importer to download a record file from one node but fail to verify it against the hash chain (because the previous file's hash is unavailable due to the partition), causing the importer to throw a HashMismatchException and permanently stall on that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the CommonDownloaderProperties sources list to be exhausted (all sources in backoff), and since RecordDownloaderProperties has no independent fallback mechanism, the record downloader silently stops downloading with no alert or automatic recovery?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the importer to be restarted by Kubernetes, and since enabled=true is the default, the importer restarts and re-downloads files from startDate, causing a thundering herd of download requests that overwhelms the cloud storage endpoint and extends the effective outage?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the importer to fail to download sidecar files after successfully downloading the main record file, and since the sidecar download failure is not propagated back to prevent the main file from being marked as processed, the database contains record items with missing sidecar data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that causes the CutoverService threshold timer (16s default) to expire during the partition, causing an automatic switch from RECORD to BLOCK stream, but since the block stream is also unavailable, both streams fail and the importer enters a permanent oscillation state between RECORD and BLOCK with no data flowing?
